artifacts.content:
From: michael.chen27@gmail.com
To: jason.chen.dev@gmail.com
Subject: Catching up + why my schedule is so weird lately

Hey Jas,

Finally have a quiet half hour – girls are in bed, Emily’s on evenings at St. Joe’s, and my back isn’t screaming for once.

You asked the other day what my week actually looks like now. Right now Daniel’s got me on this 3‑day light‑duty thing at the GreenLeaf yard on Nash Road – mostly organizing tools, fixing trimmers, and helping Sarah with scheduling. That’s what the spine clinic at Hamilton General suggested after the disc herniation they found last March when I wrecked myself lifting those 80 lb patio stones on that Stoney Creek job. The idea is to slowly build up over the next 6–9 months, but honestly I’m not sure how many hours I’ll realistically manage this year. Full‑time feels pretty up in the air.

GreenLeaf’s still a tiny outfit – good people, but it’s kind of chaos. We don’t really have proper HR or a real safety/benefits orientation for new guys. People mostly learn by tagging along and hoping they don’t grab the wrong lever on the skid steer. No surprise we burn through labourers every season once the heat and long days hit.

On the home side, Emily picked up that extra 0.5 FTE of nights back in October 2023 so we could keep up with the $1,350 on Fairview and my $280 Niagara College loan when my hours dropped. It’s brutal on her sleep, but it kept us afloat.

How are things at your place? Still splitting time between the Toronto condo and crashing here when you’ve got appointments? Let me know if you need a ride to CAMH again this month.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Catching up + why my schedule is so weird lately

artifacts.content:
Thread with: Carlos R.
Date: 2025-02-22

Mike: Yo, how are you holding up? Haven’t seen you at the yard since before Christmas.

Carlos: Still off, man. Seizure stuff + program. I’m at Hamilton Centre for Mental Health 3 days a week, 12‑week thing. Daniel’s trying to juggle the schedule.

Mike: I heard a bit from Emily after my appointment at St. Joe’s outpatient clinic. Dr. Ahmed was going over my back injury + depression notes on Jan 17 and mentioned how rough that day at the Gage Park site was for you. I’m glad you’re getting help.

Carlos: Yeah. Embarrassing though.

Mike: Nothing to be embarrassed about. Remember last July when I ended up in the ER at Hamilton General after that 60‑hour week? Full‑on panic attack, told Dr. Patel I didn’t want to be here anymore. That whole “recurrent major depressive episodes” label still freaks me out, and the meds, the GAF score stuff – all of it. You’re doing the right thing.

Carlos: How’s work with your back?

Mike: Still modified. Daniel and I agreed it’s safer if I stay on light stuff for now – shop work, some site checks – kinda indefinitely until we see how things go. I’m honestly scared if I jump back into heavy interlock or snow routes I’ll flare everything and end up off again.

Carlos: Makes sense.

Mike: On the plus side, Sophie’s loving JK at King’s Forest. She still talks about you living in our basement last winter like it was a big sleepover.

Carlos: Tell her I say hi.

Mike: Will do. Text if you need a ride to program or anything.

From: michael.chen27@gmail.com
To: lan.chen88@hotmail.com
Subject: Quick update on Dad, the girls, and money stuff

Hi Ma,

Just wanted to update you before your night shift at the store.

First, Dad – I still think about his mild stroke at Trillium in Mississauga back in March 2023. After that, and then his scare again on October 9, 2023, Emily pushed hard for us to get our own situation sorted. We went on her union family plan at St. Joe’s starting March 1, 2024, so now 80% of my duloxetine and physio at Mountain Physiotherapy Clinic are covered. We also added $250,000 of life insurance on me so if something like what happened to Ba happens to me, the Fairview mortgage is still okay.

You’ve already been helping so much – those 3–4 nights a week you spend at our place when my back flares and I’ve got physio mean we can keep Lily in her home daycare three days a week and still get Sophie to King’s Forest on time. I know you’re tired driving back and forth from the Mississauga condo.

Work is still up and down. GreenLeaf’s a good company, but we lose a lot of labourers once it gets busy or the weather turns ugly. People don’t always realize what they’re signing up for with snow, rain, and 10‑hour days.

Emily is still doing extra nights on the surgical floor, like when she bumped up her hours in October 2023 after my first leave. It’s hard on her, but between that and WSIB helping with some rehab bills at Hamilton Back & Pain Clinic, we’re managing.

We’re thinking of coming to Mississauga next Sunday if my back cooperates. I’ll text you Saturday night.

Love,
Mike

metadata.subject: Quick update on Dad, the girls, and money stuff

artifacts.content:
Title: Late-night thoughts about work & home
Date: 2025-02-05
Location: Kitchen table, 27 Fairview

Back is buzzing again tonight. Hard to tell if it’s from overdoing it at the yard or just sitting too long.

I keep thinking about how this all snowballed. First the overexertion on that condo grading job at 18 Kingfisher Drive in Stoney Creek back in July 2023 – started the disc issues. Then the August 2023 interlock job at 2210 Barton Street East where I strained it again and ended up filing that WSIB claim. Since fall 2023 Daniel’s been careful, keeping me mostly on lighter garden bed stuff and tool repairs at 1420 Industrial Park Road.

Then there was the MRI at Hamilton General in February 2024 that showed the disc bulge. Sarah shifted me into more office work – scheduling, quoting, safety paperwork – three days a week instead of full mowing and snow routes. Honestly, the modified schedule has made life with the kids easier to juggle, especially with Emily’s 12‑hour nights at St. Joe’s and Sophie’s JK drop‑offs at King’s Forest.

I still remember that November 2022 blow‑up at the Nash Road yard over the 10‑hour Oakville Saturday. Daniel’s written warning is still in my head whenever overtime comes up.

Weirdly, having Jason crash on our couch during his CBT program at CAMH last summer made me feel less alone about the mental health side of all this. And now Mom’s semi‑living here half the week to help when my pain spikes.

Need to ask Dr. Wu at Queenston Medical about different meds – the muscle relaxant from Fairview Clinic made me too drowsy to safely drive Sophie.

Title: Rehab, kids, and work – weekly juggle
Date: Week of 2025-02-17
Location: 27 Fairview / various

Mon 17 Feb
- 7:30–8:15 am: Get Sophie ready for JK (King’s Forest PS). Mark (next‑door) doing drop‑off because of drowsy back meds.
- 9:30–11:00 am: Physio – Mountain Physiotherapy on Concession (Mark Pelletier). Notes from last review (Feb 18): on track for full‑time field work by late May 2025 if I keep 30–40 min home exercises and maintain 10–15% weight loss.
- 1:00–4:00 pm: Modified shift at GreenLeaf yard (1420 Industrial Park Rd) – tool maintenance, help Sarah with schedules.

Tue 18 Feb
- 10:00–11:00 am: Hamilton Back & Pain Clinic – CBT session (WSIB claim 24‑HBP‑1317, up to $1,200/month rehab Apr 2024–Apr 2025 after May 6 Upper James lifting injury).
- 3:30–5:00 pm: Playtime with Sophie & Lily; avoid lifting >20 kg per Dr. Moroz’s Feb 6 instructions.

Wed 19 Feb
- 8:00–2:00 pm: Light‑duty yard work on Nash Road (no heavy lifting/twisting).
- 3:00–3:30 pm: Call Dr. Patel’s office at Hamilton General Spine Clinic to confirm April 9 MRI time for L4–L5 herniation.

Thu 20 Feb
- 5:30–6:30 pm: Home exercises (core/hip) while Emily sleeps before night shift at St. Joe’s.

Fri 21 Feb
- 9:00–12:00 pm: Office side – quoting/safety paperwork with Sarah.
- Evening: Budget check with Emily – rent/mortgage, $280 Niagara loan, Lily’s 3‑day daycare.

Note: Winter routes still hectic – crews rushing snow clearing/salting when we’re short staffed and trying to cover multiple sites in a tiny window.

metadata.title: Rehab, kids, and work – weekly juggle

artifacts.content:
Group chat: GreenLeaf Crew – “Backyard From Hell”
Date: 2024-09-08

Mike: My legs are still shaking from that hill on Glencrest. Whoever sold that client on a full interlock patio up there owes us beer.

Mark O.: For real. No way to get the mini‑ex in. Just us and 60 trips up and down that goat trail with base material.

Liam: My watch says we did 18k steps before lunch.

Mike: This is the stuff people don’t see. We take on these steep‑slope backyards with sketchy access, and half the time we’re hauling blocks and plate tampers by hand up uneven ground because we can’t get a machine in. No supervisor today either – we just kind of figured it out as we went so we didn’t blow the deadline.

Carlos: Welcome to GreenLeaf 😂

Mike: At least Daniel brought pizza. And he’s been good about keeping me off the worst of it since my back went on me at that Barton Street job last August. I mostly stuck to cutting pavers and checking levels.

Liam: How’s your back after today?

Mike: Sore but not like before. Physio at Wellington + the stuff at Hamilton Back & Pain Clinic is helping. Dr. Wu keeps warning me not to try to be a hero.

Mark O.: Winter’s gonna be worse. You know those condo sites where we’re salting 3 places at once with two guys.

Mike: Yeah, but Daniel said I can skip most of the overnight snow call‑outs now that Emily’s on 12‑hr nights at St. Joe’s and we can’t afford full‑time daycare. I’ll stick to daytime runs.

Carlos: Smart.

Mike: Anyway, I’m icing and then crashing. See you clowns at the yard 6:30.

From: michael.chen27@gmail.com
To: emily.zhang.rn@gmail.com
Subject: Today’s appointments + kid logistics

Hey Em,

Just writing this so we don’t forget who’s doing what tomorrow.

8:00–8:30 – I’ll handle breakfast with the girls. Because of the naproxen + muscle relaxant combo from Fairview Clinic, I still don’t feel great about driving first thing, so Mark next door will do Sophie’s drop‑off at King’s Forest again.

10:00 – I’m at Wellington Street Rehab Centre for physio. Dr. Moroz at Hamilton General’s Back & Spine Clinic said on Feb 6 that if I keep up twice‑weekly physio and don’t lift more than 20 kg until June, I should be off the prescription pain meds in 6–8 weeks.

1:30 – Follow‑up with Dr. Wu at Queenston Medical. Need to mention how bad the pain got in early December when I tried shovelling the driveway at Fairview, and ask if she still thinks full recovery in 3–4 months is realistic.

3:30 – Pick up Lily from daycare and Sophie from school (I’ll walk if my back is okay; otherwise can you grab one of them on your way home?).

6:00 – You nap before your 12‑hr night at St. Joe’s.

Also, I keep thinking about Sophie’s developmental assessment at McMaster Children’s last January after her JK teacher flagged her speech and fine motor stuff. With everything going on with my back and the depression after the Oct 21 injury, I don’t want to miss any follow‑up for her.

We still need to go over the budget tonight – mortgage, $280 student loan, Lily’s 3‑day daycare – before your extra 0.5 FTE of nights kicks in again next month.

I’ll start dinner (something easy, maybe stir‑fry) before your shift.

– M

From: Michael Chen <m.chen27@example.com>
To: Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@example.com>
Subject: Notes before tomorrow’s benefits call
Date: 2025-05-12

Hey Em,

Before we talk to Daniel and Sarah about this new group benefits quote for GreenLeaf Urban Landscaping Inc., I wanted to get everything straight.

You remember how messy it’s been:

- Back in Spring 2021, when I was on anxiety and depression leave and seeing Mark Ellis at the Hamilton Community Mental Health Centre every Thursday at 5:30 p.m., Sun Life initially denied my short‑term disability claim as “pre‑existing.” That’s why I hesitated to say “yes” on the form about always having coverage.

- Then on July 1, 2023, when our Sun Life plan renewed, there was that lapse before Daniel set up the temporary plan in August 2023. Because of that gap, my physio at Lifemark Physiotherapy on Queenston Road wasn’t covered and we got stuck with the $1,200 bill. We had to start a 12‑month payment plan on August 15, 2023. I’m worried something like that could happen again.

- On top of that, remember Lily’s birth? When you had the emergency C‑section at St. Joseph’s Healthcare Hamilton on May 6, 2022, we found out at admitting that my family coverage hadn’t been updated properly after Sophie. For the first three months, Lily’s hospital and pediatric bills weren’t fully covered until Sun Life fixed it in August 2022.

All this makes me nervous about admin errors and gaps in coverage, especially now that my back and mental health are so tied to work.

Let’s review this tonight after the girls are asleep.

Love,
Michael

From: Michael Chen <m.chen27@example.com>
To: Jason Chen <jason.chen@example.com>
Subject: Catch‑up on the whole work/health mess
Date: 2025-05-14

Hey Jas,

Figured I’d write everything out instead of sending a bunch of scattered texts.

Physically, it’s been one thing after another. The latest flare started in early April 2025 when I twisted loading patio stones into the GreenLeaf truck at the yard. It’s an acute‑on‑chronic lumbar strain now – pain shoots down my right leg, muscle spasms, limited range of motion. I even missed Sophie’s kindergarten events at King’s Forest Public School. Mom (Lan Chen) came down from Mississauga and stayed with us at 27 Fairview Avenue for 3 weeks to help with Lily because I can’t safely lift her or stand long enough to do bath time.

This is on top of the February 20, 2025 incident where I moved a full pallet of patio stones on a GreenLeaf Urban Landscaping job site. Daniel McLeod filed the incident report the following week and WSIB opened a claim, but the adjuster is already questioning whether my physio back in 2019 at the Niagara College horticulture facilities makes this “pre‑existing.”

You know this isn’t new – the first big lumbar strain was in 2018 helping Dad (Wei Chen) move heavy machinery at his shop in Mississauga. Then the October 21, 2024 workplace injury kicked off the anxiety and sleep issues. On February 3, 2025, my psychologist, Dr. Laura Bennett at St. Joseph’s Healthcare Hamilton Outpatient Mental Health Clinic, wrote that my prognosis for returning to full‑time work is good if I stick with biweekly CBT sessions for another 3 months.

I’m trying to stay hopeful, but the back stuff and paperwork are exhausting.

– Mike

metadata.subject: Catch‑up on the whole work/health mess

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Chen <m.chen27@example.com>
To: Personal File
Subject: Timeline for insurance/household stuff (for forms)
Date: 2025-05-16

Saving this for myself so I don’t have to keep rewriting it on every form.

Household/relationships: My spouse is Emily Zhang and we live together at 27 Fairview Avenue, Hamilton, ON with our daughters, Sophie (5) and Lily (2). We had an informal separation trial and family court mediation that wrapped up in February 2024 at John Sopinka Courthouse. The family court mediator advised against a shared‑household arrangement because of my depression and panic attacks after my back injury and modified‑duty leave from GreenLeaf Urban Landscaping Inc., so the kids stay full‑time with us here.

My in‑laws don’t normally live with us, but Emily’s mom stayed in our spare room from November 15 to December 5, 2023 (three‑week stay) at 27 Fairview after my minor oxycodone relapse. My family doctor at the Hamilton Family Health Team clinic flagged the relapse while I was off on modified/medical leave, and her staying with us from Markham was basically a temporary part of our household to help with recovery and childcare.

Prior insurance issues: Our business has had a prior group benefits plan declined. In August 2023, Co‑operators declined Daniel McLeod’s application for GreenLeaf Urban Landscaping Inc. because their underwriter flagged my July 2023 Hamilton General Hospital emergency room visit for a panic attack and suicidal ideation after a 60‑hour workweek, plus two previous WSIB claims in 2021 and 2022 related to my chronic lumbar disc issues. The decline letter specifically referenced my GAF score from my psychiatrist, Dr. Patel, and noted concern about “recurrent major depressive episodes” requiring ongoing SSRI treatment.

Keep this handy for WSIB, insurers, and any new benefits applications.

– Michael Jiahao Chen

From: Michael Chen <michael.chen@example.com>
To: Emily Zhang <emily.zhang@example.com>
Date: May 6, 2025 8:14 PM
Subject: Dr. Patel’s plan + what the next few weeks look like

Hey Em,

Just got back from St. Joseph’s Healthcare Hamilton and wanted to write everything down before I forget.

Dr. Patel went over the MRI and exam and confirmed it’s a soft tissue lumbar strain again. He’s linking it directly to the GreenLeaf Urban Landscaping job site in Hamilton on May 3, 2025, when we were lifting those 80 lb patio stones at the small plaza. He said that lift probably aggravated the old lumbar strain from 2018, when I helped Dad (Wei Chen) move that heavy machinery at his shop in Mississauga.

He’s set up a 12‑week physiotherapy program for me: twice‑weekly sessions at the clinic and daily exercises at home. Official restrictions are:
- No lifting more than 10 kg
- No standing longer than 30 minutes at a time without a break

He was pretty clear I have to stick to this if I want a full recovery and to get back to GreenLeaf by late summer. I told him about doing school drop‑off for Sophie and daycare runs with Lily, and he said it’s okay as long as I’m careful and don’t pick them up the old way. Lots of bending at the knees, no twisting.

Can you plug the twice‑weekly sessions into our shared calendar? I’m thinking mid‑morning slots so traffic is lighter and my back is warmed up.

We’ll have to look at the budget again, but he said WSIB should accept this plan.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Dr. Patel’s plan + what the next few weeks look like

artifacts.content:
Personal Rehab Notes – Michael Chen

Date: May 10, 2025
Location: Kitchen table, 27 Fairview Avenue, Hamilton

Summary of injury:
- Worksite lifting incident on May 3, 2025 at a GreenLeaf Urban Landscaping job site in Hamilton.
- Strained lower back lifting 80 lb patio stones.
- This aggravated the initial lumbar strain from 2018 while helping Dad (Wei Chen) move machinery at his Mississauga shop.

Medical plan (per Dr. Patel, St. Joseph’s Healthcare Hamilton):
- Diagnosis: work-related lower back soft tissue injury.
- Start of 12‑week physiotherapy rehabilitation program this week.
- Twice‑weekly sessions booked (Tues/Fri, mid‑morning).
- Restrictions: 10 kg lifting restriction; 30 minutes standing limit before I must sit or stretch.

Daily observations:
- Pain increases after standing 25–30 mins at the counter (making Sophie’s lunches, dishes).
- Walking to King’s Forest Public School is okay if I keep pace slow and use proper posture.
- Need to kneel to hug/pick up Lily instead of bending from the waist.

Work/WSIB notes:
- On modified/medical leave from GreenLeaf; Daniel and Sarah need clear updates.
- Goal: respect 10 kg limit on any light duties (no shovels full of soil, no lifting fertilizer bags).

Reminder to self:
Stick to the twice‑weekly sessions and home exercises. The 12‑week physiotherapy program is my best shot at getting back to full duties safely and avoiding another flare‑up like 2018.
